long_description: In-flight loss of stable engine combustion resulting in rapid thrust decay or complete flameout during maneuvering, climb, or high-altitude operation.
pilot_actions: Set appropriate airstart conditions, attempt relight per checklist, minimize maneuvering, and prepare for diversion or ejection if relight fails.

long_description: In-flight FADEC malfunction causes uncommanded thrust changes, degraded throttle response, protective mode entry, or engine limit exceedance warnings.
pilot_actions: Stabilize throttle inputs, avoid abrupt power changes, monitor engine indications, select standby or degraded mode if available, and recover to base.

long_description: Fuel system leak develops in flight, causing abnormal fuel quantity decrease, possible imbalance, and elevated fire risk.
pilot_actions: Monitor fuel state and imbalance, shut off affected feed path if possible, avoid afterburner, declare emergency, and divert to nearest suitable airfield.

long_description: In-flight fuel feed pressure drops below engine demand, increasing risk of surge, stall, or flameout during high-power operation.
pilot_actions: Reduce power demand, avoid negative-g or aggressive maneuvering, monitor engine response, and recover using the shortest safe route.

long_description: The main generator fails in flight, forcing transfer to battery or emergency buses and reducing available electrical loads.
pilot_actions: Confirm power source transfer, shed nonessential avionics, minimize sensor and mission loads, and land before backup power margins are exhausted.

long_description: An in-flight main electrical bus fault causes intermittent or sustained loss of power to multiple downstream avionics or aircraft systems.
pilot_actions: Reset affected bus only if checklist permits, prioritize essential flight instruments, disable nonessential equipment, and terminate the mission.

long_description: A primary flight control actuator jams in flight, causing asymmetric control response, restricted maneuver authority, or increased pilot workload.
pilot_actions: Stop aggressive inputs, hold a controllable flight regime, use trim and remaining control authority, and recover for a straight-in landing if feasible.

long_description: Air data or flight control sensing becomes unreliable in flight, producing false speed, altitude, or control-law cues.
pilot_actions: Cross-check standby instruments, revert to alternate or degraded control mode if available, fly conservative pitch-power references, and divert.

long_description: An in-flight hydraulic leak reduces available fluid and pressure, threatening powered flight controls, landing gear actuation, or braking.
pilot_actions: Monitor pressure trend, conserve hydraulic demand, avoid unnecessary configuration changes, and plan landing with alternate extension procedures.

long_description: Hydraulic pressure drops in flight because of pump degradation or internal leakage, causing sluggish or partial system response.
pilot_actions: Reduce maneuver loads, maintain stable airspeed, confirm backup system status, and recover before further hydraulic degradation occurs.

long_description: The radar processor computer fails in flight, leading to loss of radar modes, unreliable tracks, or tactical display corruption.
pilot_actions: Recycle radar mode if permitted, disregard suspect radar tracks, transition to visual or alternate sensor tactics, and continue only if mission rules allow.

long_description: The navigation computer degrades or fails in flight, causing invalid position, steering, or timing data to the mission system.
pilot_actions: Cross-check backup navigation sources, avoid reliance on coupled steering modes, switch to alternate navigation mode if available, and divert if positional confidence is lost.

long_description: Cockpit pressurization is lost in flight, increasing hypoxia risk and reducing safe altitude margin.
pilot_actions: Don oxygen immediately, initiate emergency descent as required, limit mission continuation, and recover at a breathable altitude.

long_description: In-flight ECS cooling shortfall causes avionics bay overheating, increasing the risk of computer resets or mission system shutdowns.
pilot_actions: Reduce nonessential avionics load, monitor overheat indications, avoid prolonged high-heat operating modes, and recover before cascading avionics failures occur.

long_description: Landing gear indicates unsafe status in flight after retraction or during approach setup, with possible partial extension or uplock failure.
pilot_actions: Hold to troubleshoot per checklist, attempt alternate gear cycling if permitted, prepare for abnormal landing, and coordinate emergency services.

long_description: The display processor computer fails in flight, causing loss or corruption of head-down display pages and caution formatting.
pilot_actions: Transfer attention to standby instruments and remaining displays, declutter nonessential pages, and terminate tactical tasking if situational awareness is degraded.

long_description: The mission management computer suffers an in-flight processing or software fault, degrading sensor fusion, mode management, and tactical workflow coordination.
pilot_actions: Freeze configuration changes if the system is unstable, cross-check data on remaining displays, simplify mission tasking, and recover if core mission logic is unreliable.

long_description: The stores management computer fails in flight, causing unreliable weapon station status, release inhibit issues, or incorrect inventory presentation.
pilot_actions: Safe the armament system as required, avoid weapon employment from suspect stations, confirm remaining stores status conservatively, and continue only within safety constraints.

long_description: The communication control computer fails in flight, disrupting radio tuning, data link routing, or communication status reporting.
pilot_actions: Select backup radio paths if available, reduce reliance on data link coordination, use prebriefed comm procedures, and recover if communication loss compromises mission safety.
